back_translation: In the early dawn of the first day of the week, the women took the spices they had prepared to the tomb.
They found that the stone had been rolled away from the tomb.
When they entered, they did not find the body.
While they were puzzling over this, behold, two men in shining garments came and stood beside them.
When they became terrified and bowed their faces to the ground, the men said to them, 'Why do you seek the One who lives among the dead?'
He is not here, but He has risen. Remember what He said to you when He was still in the region of Galilee,
saying, 'The Son of Man must be handed over into the grasp of sinful people, must be crucified on a cross, and on the third day He will rise.'
Then they remembered His words.
When they returned from the tomb, they went and reported all these things to the eleven disciples and to all the others.
Those women were Mary of Magdala, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women who were with them, who told these things to the apostles.
But these words appeared like nonsense in their eyes, and so they did not believe them.
But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. When he stooped down to look, he saw only the linen cloths lying alone, and he returned wondering at what had happened.
Behold, on that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
They were conversing with each other about all the events that had happened.
While they were conversing and debating together, Jesus Himself drew near and walked along with them.
But their eyes were held shut, keeping them from recognizing Him.
He said to them, 'What are you exchanging back-and-forth about while walking along?' They stopped and stood still, their faces sorrowful.
One of them, named Cleopas, answered Him, 'Are you the only one who lives in Jerusalem and does not know about the things that have happened in this city during these past days?'
He asked, 'What things?' They said to Him, 'The things about Jesus of Nazareth, who was a mighty prophet both in deeds and in words before the face of God and before all the people.
And how our chief priests and rulers handed Him over to be judged with the death-sentence, and crucified Him on the cross.
But we had been hoping that He was the one who would redeem Israel. Moreover, today is already the third day since these things happened.
Moreover, some women in our group also astonished us, because they went to the tomb very early in the morning.
When they did not find His body, they came and told us that they had seen a vision of angels, who said that He is alive.
Some of us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but did not see Him.'
He said to them, 'O foolish ones and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!
Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer in this way before entering into His glory?'
Then He began to explain to them, starting from the writings of Moses and all the prophets — explaining the passages concerning Himself in all the Scriptures.
When they approached the village they were going to, He acted as though He would travel on further.
But they earnestly urged Him, saying, 'Please stay with us, for it is near evening and the day is almost gone.' So He went in to stay with them.
While He was reclining at the meal with them, He took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them.
Then their eyes were brightened, and they recognized Him. But He vanished from their sight.
They said to each other, 'Were not our hearts burning within us while He spoke with us along the way and opened the Scriptures to us?'
In that same hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem, and they found the eleven disciples and those who were with them gathered together.
Who were saying, 'The Lord has truly risen, and He has appeared to Simon!'
Then the two disciples recounted what had happened along the way, and how they had recognized Him when He broke the bread.
While they were telling these things, He Himself came and stood in their midst.
They were startled and afraid, thinking they had seen a spirit.
He said to them, 'Why are you agitated in your hearts? And why does doubt arise in your minds?
Look at My hands and feet — it is I Myself. Take hold of Me and examine, for a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have.'
When He had said this, He showed them His hands and feet.
While they still did not believe, because of joy and amazement, He said to them, 'Do you have anything here to eat?'
They offered Him a piece of broiled fish.
He took it and ate in front of them.

He said to them, 'These are the words I spoke to you when I was still with you — that everything written about Me in the Law of Moses, in the books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms, must be fulfilled.'
Then He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.
He said to them, 'According to what is written, the Christ must suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
and there will be a proclamation in His name of repentance for the forgiveness of sins to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
You are witnesses of these things.
Behold, I will send the promise of My Father upon you. But you stay in this city first, until you receive power from on high clothing your body.'
He led them out as far as the village of Bethany, and lifted up His hand to bless them.
While He was blessing them, He parted from them and was taken up into heaven.
They knelt and worshiped Him, then returned to Jerusalem with exceedingly great joy.
And they stayed in the temple continually, praising God.
